input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. Use `DynamicResource`, not `StaticResource`**

`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML parse time and never updates. Replace it with `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the merged dictionary:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when the resource value changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by replacing the merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Prevent activity restart on Android config change**

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Handle it yourself in `Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.UiMode | ConfigChanges.Orientation | ConfigChanges.ScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

Adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` tells Android your app will handle the change — no restart. You can then detect the system toggle and apply your theme:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);
    var isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your DynamicResource dictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app regarding dark mode:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but when they switch themes at runtime, nothing changes.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode is toggled** — When the user changes dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts completely.

Let me address both issues:

**Issue 1: StaticResource vs DynamicResource**
- `StaticResource` is resolved once at XAML load time and never updates. To get runtime theme changes, they need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which re-evaluates when the resource dictionary changes.


- For the second issue, Android automatically destroys and recreates the Activity when the UI mode configuration changes. I can prevent this by adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to the Activity attribute in MainActivity, which tells Android to handle the configuration change without restarting the app.
